resources.string: Some permissions not granted, may affect functionality
Permission permanently denied, please enable in settings
TinaIDE needs storage permission to access and manage your project files.
Please grant storage permission for editor functionality.
Please enable "All files access" in system settings

If you previously saved projects in the public directory (Documents/TinaIDE), grant file-management permission first. After that, the project list will refresh automatically and you can open those projects directly.
No projects yet, create new or choose directory from top-right menu
Select a file from the file tree to start editing
Tip: Swipe right from left edge to open file tree
Project name can only contain letters, numbers, underscores and hyphens
Plugin project created. Run validates and hot-installs it; Package creates a .tinaplug.
Click the + button at bottom right to quickly create your first project
Font, Tab, line wrap and other editor behaviors
Optimization, build parallelism, CMake configuration, etc.
Auto-save, auto-backup and other project management options
Terminal theme, font size, interaction behavior
Manage installed Linux and Android libraries
Version info, GitHub, open source licenses
Generate an Ed25519 SSH key pair
Import an existing SSH private key file
Used when host binding is not set
Choose a key for a host (e.g. github.com)
Go to "Market" on the home screen to browse and install plugins, or use the menu above to install from file
Reload the manifest and main script without restarting the IDE
Run Plugin Doctor again against the current installed plugin directory
Open the log panel filtered to this plugin
Switch to another source to inspect load, health check, or runtime issues.
Switch to another diagnostics filter or source to inspect why plugins were not loaded.
LSP dependency issues were detected. You can repair them directly.
Contributions: themes %1$d · file tree menus %2$d · editor menus %3$d
"%1$s" has errors, so installation was blocked. Fix them and select the plugin package again.
"%1$s" has warnings. Review the risks before continuing installation.
Check manifest.json basics, apiVersion, main path, networkHosts, and resource paths first.
Check that permissions / optionalPermissions use TinaIDE-supported permission IDs, and remove duplicates.
Check that commands, menus, fileIcons, projectTemplates, and other contributions are complete, and referenced commands/files exist.
Check whether apiVersion, minAppVersion, or plugin type matches the current IDE support range.
This is a runtime issue. Check plugin logs, script entrypoints, and permission grants first.
This is an LSP dependency issue. Open the plugin details, review error logs, and repair dependencies.
Check logs and stack traces, fix the script error, then reload the plugin manually.
Required LSP toolchains are not installed: %1$s
Required LSP toolchains failed to install: %1$s
Install LSP dependencies, then reopen the file.
Review error logs, repair LSP dependencies, then reopen the file.
Declare the required permission in manifest, grant it in the permissions view, then retry. If it still fails, inspect logs or reload the plugin manually.
Are you sure you want to uninstall “%1$s”?
This plugin requests high-risk permissions and may access system files or execute commands. Please ensure you trust this plugin source.
Reinstall failed or missing toolchains required by language server
Install toolchains required by language server
Language server dependencies passed checks and can start directly.
Dependency or startup failures were detected. Review error logs and run repair.
Language server dependencies are not installed yet. Install them first.
This LSP plugin declares no extra toolchains. Startup state depends on server probing.
"%1$s" requires the following toolchains to provide code completion:
"%1$s" has failed LSP dependencies. TinaIDE will re-check and install missing or failed toolchains:
Linux environment is available. System package manager: %1$s
Linux environment is unavailable. Install and enable a Linux distro first.
System package installation is unavailable: active package manager is not supported (%1$s)
Required LSP toolchains are not installed: %1$s
Required LSP toolchains failed to install: %1$s
LSP startup command was not found: %1$s. Install plugin dependencies first.
The active Linux distro failed self-check. Missing required items: %1$s
Cannot detect the package manager for the active Linux distro
Extract target path is not configured
Choose editor color scheme, gray themes can reduce eye strain
Use GPU rendering for better scrolling performance. Disable if you experience stuttering or black screen (requires reopening files)
Limit the number of candidates returned by clangd. Higher values use more resources, restart LSP to apply changes.
Debug session not created yet. Click the \"Debug\" button above the editor to start.
I have read and agree to the User Agreement and Privacy Policy
This is a project exported from TinaIDE: %1$s
Project is empty, no files to export
Are you sure you want to delete project \"%1$s\"?\n\nThis will permanently delete the project folder and all its contents, and cannot be undone.
Bundled plugins cannot be uninstalled as they are essential system components.
All selected plugins are bundled and cannot be uninstalled.
Click the button at bottom right to create a new project
Choosing a folder copies it into %1$s. Choosing an archive extracts it into %1$s.
This folder cannot be opened directly as a project. Please choose a real local storage folder.
The archive is empty and cannot be imported
Project name can only contain letters, numbers, underscores and hyphens
Cloning large repositories may take a long time, please ensure stable network connection.
Please enter a valid Git repository URL
Are you sure you want to delete project \"%1$s\"?
This will permanently delete the project folder and all its contents, and cannot be undone.
You can continue browsing the project; build features will be available after installation completes.
💡 Tip: Download failure may be due to network issues, try switching to a domestic mirror source.
Suitable for simple programs and learning
Ready-to-run SDL3 app with callback entry point and window demo
Android NDK shared library project with custom API level
Select the C++ language standard version for your project
Choose a TinaIDE plugin template to start development
After creation, Run validates manifest first, then hot-installs the plugin.
Loaded from the user template folder; project name placeholders are replaced during creation.
After creation, open the project and tap Run to validate, package, and hot-install it. Tap Package to create a .tinaplug for sharing or install-from-file.
There are no templates available for project creation. Please try again later.
Make sure TinaIDE Plugin Starters is installed and enabled, then open the plugin tutorial entry again.
Install and use Zsh in the current Linux system
Install git, curl, cmake, and basic build tools in one step. Packages are selected automatically for the current Linux distribution.
Using non-English locales like Chinese requires locale support inside the PRoot Linux environment.\n\nInstall now? (Network connection required)

This will install common development packages in the current Linux system, such as git, curl, cmake, and basic build tools.\n\nThe exact packages are selected automatically based on the current distribution. Install now? (Network connection required)
This will reinstall the current Linux development packages to repair missing files or broken dependencies.\n\nContinue? (Network connection required)
This will reinstall Zsh in the current Linux system to repair missing files or broken dependencies.\n\nContinue? (Network connection required)
This will reinstall and rebuild locale support for the current Linux system locale: %1$s.\n\nContinue? (Network connection required)
Invalid font file, please select TTF or OTF format
Data preserved after uninstall, accessible via computer
No permissions needed, data deleted after uninstall
Best performance, data deleted after uninstall
Import projects from sync target to private workspace
Export projects from private workspace to sync target
Allow MT Manager to access app private directory (no ROOT required)
Reclaim space from build outputs, caches, and logs
All files in this category will be deleted. This cannot be undone. Continue?
Caches, build intermediates, and logs in every category will be removed. Source code, project configuration, and fonts are not affected. Continue?
Some files could not be removed (%1$d). Freed %2$s
build/ directory under each project workspace, regenerated on next build
Scratch files created by PRoot at runtime
Cached downloads (rootfs, etc.); will be re-downloaded if reinstalled
Temporary files produced while exporting or sharing a project
Build logs and PRoot session logs
Logs from installing the PRoot environment and dependencies
Nothing to clean in this category
%1$d item(s) totalling %2$s will be deleted. This cannot be undone. Continue?
Automatically backup project files before compilation
Only affects future new projects and can be temporarily overridden in the wizard.
Source files remain after uninstall, but creating projects requires file-management permission.
No file permission required and builds are more reliable, but projects are removed when the app is uninstalled.
Set the APK export type for this project.
Re-scan this project and refresh APK export support.
Open a project first, then configure Include/Library/Runtime paths
Append header search paths for compile (-I)
Append library search paths (-L) and auto link scan
Append dynamic library search paths for runtime (LD_LIBRARY_PATH)
One path per line. Supports absolute paths and paths relative to project root.
Append to CMake configure stage (one arg per line)
Input native compile/link flags. Multi-line is supported and will be merged by spaces when saved.
One CMake argument per line. Example: -DXXX=ON
Reset all shortcuts to default values
This shortcut is already used by \"%1$s\"
Are you sure you want to restore all shortcuts to default settings?
Tip: Hardware keyboard required to set shortcuts
Go back to the previous code navigation location
Go forward to the next code navigation location
Preview the current symbol definition without leaving the current location
Go to the current symbol definition
Find references to the current symbol
Go to the current symbol type definition
Go to the current symbol implementation
Show code actions for the current cursor or selection
Switch between C/C++ header and source files
Show line numbers on the left side of editor
Wrap lines when exceeding screen width
Color brackets based on nesting depth
Auto-disable rainbow brackets when exceeding this line count (0 = unlimited)
Allow folding/expanding code blocks (functions, classes, etc.)
Native mode has better performance, PRoot mode has better compatibility
Analyze project files for better cross-file completion (uses more resources)
Enable code quality checks with warnings and suggestions
Insert argument placeholders when completing functions
Changes require reopening files or restarting LSP to take effect
Automatically add indentation on new lines
Insert spaces instead of tab characters when pressing Tab

Scope: these are global defaults. Optimization and threads apply to single-file/Make; CMake build type, generator, and parallel jobs apply only to CMake. Compiler/toolchain/output mode are set in Run Configuration.
Import custom toolchain from external file
Import system root directory from external file
Invalid toolchain archive, bin/clang not found
No toolchain configured. Please install or import a toolchain first.
Toolchain sha256 file is empty or unreadable: %1$s
Toolchain root directory not found after extraction
Native mode has better performance, PRoot mode has better compatibility
Native mode is faster, PRoot mode has better compatibility
Native mode is faster, PRoot mode has better compatibility
Are you sure you want to redeploy the development environment? This will reinstall the PRoot toolchain and may take several minutes.
Choose the overall color scheme of the app
Set the display position of debug toolbar
Refresh failed, showing the last successful data
Long press or click button to copy ID
If TinaIDE helps you, scan a code to support project maintenance.
View the open source components and licenses used by this app
TinaIDE supports three sync modes, please choose based on your usage habits and needs.
Data preserved after uninstall, accessible via computer
No permissions needed, visible in system file manager
Best performance, uses private directory only
Data Safety: User data preserved after uninstall
File Visible: Easy access for third-party apps
Cross-device: Access project files via computer
No Permission: Compatible with all Android versions
File Visible: Accessible in system file manager
Small Storage: Only one copy of data
Note: Data will be lost after uninstall
Note: Sync mode can be changed in settings
Skipping will have the following effects:
Projects saved only in app internal directory
Project data may be lost after uninstalling the app
Cannot access projects directly via file manager
Suggestion: Go back to select workspace directory for full functionality
Installation paused. You can resume anytime.
First-time installation may take longer. Please wait.
Almost done. Finishing up the last steps.
If download is slow, click the \"Switch\" button above to select another mirror source.
Compilation toolchain and runtime environment have been successfully deployed.
Download failure may be due to network issues, try switching mirror source and retry.
Please uninstall the current app and install the APK that matches your device architecture
Please check network connection or storage space and retry
Parent directory does not exist or is not a directory
Source file does not exist or is not a file
Source directory does not exist or is not a directory
File does not exist or is not a file
Out of memory, please close other apps and try again
WeChat version does not support this feature
PRoot environment not installed/ready, please deploy development environment in settings first
Opening folders with external apps is not supported
Run in PRoot virtual Linux environment
Name can only contain letters, numbers and underscores, and cannot start with a number
Folder with same name already exists
File or folder with same name already exists
Are you sure you want to delete folder \"%1$s\" and all its contents?\n\nThis action cannot be undone.
Are you sure you want to delete file \"%1$s\"?\n\nThis action cannot be undone.
Failed to process image (try another or use a smaller image)
Tool “%1$s” may perform dangerous operations. Continue?
This operation is extremely dangerous. Please confirm carefully.

Here is a summary of the previous conversation:

%1$s

Please continue based on this summary.
This will recursively delete the directory and all its contents. This action cannot be undone.
This will delete the file. This action cannot be undone.
This will delete lines from the file. This action cannot be undone.
- Use the \'site\' parameter to search a specific site
GitHub API rate limit exceeded. Please try again later or use a GitHub token.
Invalid search query. Please check your query syntax.
Network error: Unable to reach the search service. Please check your internet connection.
Network timeout: Search request timed out. Please try again.
Invalid repository format. Use owner/repo (e.g., torvalds/linux).
Failed to read GitHub file. The file may not exist, or GitHub API rate limit may be exceeded. Please try again later and check the repository and file path.
Content truncated. File has %1$d lines total.
Use a larger max_lines value to see more, or view the full file at:
AI service returned an empty response
AI service returned an empty stream response
AI stream ended without parseable output
No user or AI message to retry
Cancelled: previous tool did not complete successfully
Check your network connection and server address configuration
Check your network connection or try again later
Check the server address and port configuration
Check the server certificate or use HTTP
The server returned data in an invalid format
Check the server address and model configuration
Try again later or upgrade your account quota
Server issue, please try again later

I can help you:
• Explain code
• Generate code snippets
• Fix bugs
• Suggest refactoring
Are you sure you want to delete this conversation?
This will summarize the current conversation and create a new one. Continue?
Tool executed, click to continue AI response
Tool already executed, cannot execute again
The official AI gateway is not bundled in the open-source build
The official AI gateway is not bundled in the open-source build
No custom channels yet. Tap below to add one.
Delete \"%1$s\"? You will need to add it again.
Leave empty to keep the existing key
Base URL must start with http:// or https://
Please pick or add a channel in Settings → Channel management.
The official AI gateway is not bundled in the open-source build. Add a custom channel in Settings.

Your API Key is encrypted and stored locally. It will never be uploaded.

Get your API Key:
• DeepSeek: platform.deepseek.com
• OpenAI: platform.openai.com
• Qwen: dashscope.console.aliyun.com
Allow the model to call TinaIDE built-in tools (requires gateway tools/tool_calls support)
In auto mode, allow automatic execution of dangerous tools (e.g., file deletion, system commands)
When enabled, AI will perform deeper thinking and analysis (may increase response time)
View and manage AI tool enabled states
Select a file from the sidebar to start editing, or click
the button below to browse your project file tree.
Current file does not support search
You can reopen it from the project home page. Session state is preserved by default.
Debug start failed: executable path not found
Terminal run failed: command not found
Legacy configuration detected and migrated automatically (project.json %1$d item(s), run_configs.json %2$d item(s))
Build complete: this project has no directly runnable output, and the artifact was exported
Build complete: this project produced a %1$s and cannot be run directly
Build complete: this project produced a %1$s, cannot be run directly, and the artifact was exported
Compilation complete, artifact type: %1$s, artifact exported
Current file does not support formatting
Current file type does not support formatting
clang-format not available, please install compiler toolchain first
Reached display limit (%1$d lines), consider using hex view or opening in editor.
Failed to load open source license data: %1$s

This page shows the app\'s direct dependencies and selected bundled open source components. For readability, some AndroidX / Kotlin baseline libraries are intentionally collapsed from the list.
If you need to verify lower-level source or native submodules, please also check the LICENSE / NOTICE files shipped with the repository.
Terminal emulation and view module used by the in-app terminal (bundled with the app).
Single-header JSON library used by the app\'s native code.
Keep the editor running in the background to prevent being killed
Editor is running in the background
Android sysroot is missing or incomplete: %1$s. Please install or sync the sysroot in toolchain settings.
Android sysroot is missing a required header: %1$s
Android sysroot is missing the libc++ header: %1$s
Android sysroot is missing the API %1$d library directory: %2$s. Adjust sysrootApiLevel or reinstall the sysroot.
%1$s Conclusion: phased commands succeeded; issue is in the original combined command chain
Summarize the key points of the conversation briefly and clearly.
=== File truncated (tail only) === name=%1$s size=%2$d bytes kept=%3$d bytes
Default signing is unavailable. Place debug.keystore in assets/apk_templates/.
Parent directory does not exist or is not a directory
Source file does not exist or is not a file
Source directory does not exist or is not a directory
Sync your projects and settings to the cloud, access your code anytime, anywhere.
Automatically download and manage project dependencies, supports multiple package managers.
Intelligent code completion to improve coding efficiency.
Built-in Git support for easy version control management.
Integrated terminal to execute commands directly in the IDE.
Edit multiple files simultaneously to improve productivity.
Customize editor themes and color schemes.
Install and manage plugins to extend IDE functionality.
Prefer installing versioned Ubuntu packages (e.g. clang-19); automatically fall back on failure
Upload crash logs automatically on next launch (you can disable here)
No crash upload status has been recorded yet.
Automatic upload is off. New TinaIDE crash logs will not be uploaded automatically.
Modern C/C++ compiler with friendly diagnostics
GNU Compiler Collection with good compatibility
LLVM linker with faster linking speed
. Please check and fix the code.
Please check for missing libraries or symbol conflicts
Please open the app and wait for automatic installation to complete
Please try again later (automatic installation on first launch/update)
Please restart the app and wait for background update to complete
Please redeploy development environment in settings or try again later
Path %1$s is not in allowed range
Please ensure file path is within project directory or workspace
Please check network connection and try again
DNS hijacking or network anomaly detected
Suggest switching to mobile data or changing network environment
Please check network connection or try again later
Create any type of blank file
All required files and directories are present
Cannot be empty (compiler path in PRoot environment)
Custom compiler unavailable: Please ensure the path exists and is executable in PRoot environment
Use the first executable target in CMakeLists.txt
Use the first shared library target (.so) in CMakeLists.txt
Compile the currently open file (if it\'s a C/C++ source file)
Always compile the currently open file
Applies only to single-file builds. Follow project default first uses project settings, then falls back to C++17.
Leave empty to prefer project API (fallback 28)
Used only for native builds (CMake/Make/Single File), range 21-35. When empty, TinaIDE first reads nativeApiLevel from project metadata, then falls back to 28.
API Level must be between %1$d and %2$d
Variables supported, type $ to see available variables
Scope: this dialog configures per-run overrides (compiler, toolchain, sysroot, args, output mode). Global build defaults are in Settings → Compiler.
• Click run button: Run in output panel\n• Long press run button: Choose to run in terminal
You can use the following variables in command line arguments and working directory:
Tap %1$d more times to enable developer options
Please enter a port number between 1-65535
Module name cannot contain spaces or slashes
Tip: 127.0.0.1/localhost is the phone itself; to connect to PC, use PC LAN IP, or run adb reverse tcp:port tcp:port (use 10.0.2.2 for emulator).
Tip: 127.0.0.1/localhost is the phone itself; to connect to PC, use PC LAN IP, or run adb reverse tcp:%1$d tcp:%1$d (use 10.0.2.2 for emulator).
Tip: System blocks cleartext ws:// connections (CLEARTEXT). Please use wss://, or enable cleartext network in app (usesCleartextTraffic / networkSecurityConfig).
Please enter IP address or domain name (without protocol prefix)
Need to reactivate in About page after disabling
LLDB unavailable (may not be installed or Python bindings missing), please install/fix debug toolchain first
Current frame has no return address (e.g. exit), cannot step out, changed to continue
\n\n... [Log too long, truncated for display, click copy to get full content] ...
PRoot environment not installed, please call initialize() first
Checking available Ubuntu versioned packages (will auto-downgrade on failure)...
Current software source does not support available clang/llvm versioned packages, tried: %1$s
Unable to verify clang version after toolchain installation
No available Shell found in PRoot environment
No available Shell found in system
Please install zsh/bash, or manually select an available Shell in settings.
Please install Zsh in settings, or switch to Bash/Sh.
Please install Bash, or switch to Sh/Auto-detect.
Please check if system/environment provides sh.
Automatic Zsh installation is not supported for the current Linux distribution: %1$s
PRoot environment not installed, please call initialize() first
Checking available LLDB versioned packages (will auto-downgrade on failure)...
Current software source does not support available LLDB versioned packages, tried: %1$s
LLDB post-installation verification failed (Python bindings may be missing)
ssh-agent not running (please start it first)
Does not appear to be SSH private key content (missing PRIVATE KEY header)
PRoot environment not installed: Please install/initialize the Linux distro environment in Settings → Compiler
Key name can only contain letters/numbers/._- (and cannot contain paths)
SSH key not found: %1$s (Please generate/import in Settings → Git SSH and set as default or bind to %2$s)
This SSH private key requires a passphrase: %1$s
Automatic locale configuration is not supported for the current Linux distribution: %1$s
Automatic development package installation is not supported for the current Linux distribution: %1$s
Plugin ID cannot contain path separator: %1$s
Permission denied for tina.%1$s.%2$s: %3$s is not declared in manifest
Permission denied for tina.%1$s.%2$s: %3$s is not granted
Add manifest.json at the plugin root, then reinstall or reload the plugin.
Check manifest fields, declared permissions, and referenced resource paths, then reinstall or reload the plugin.
Keep the same permission in either permissions or optionalPermissions, not both.
networkHosts is declared, but neither network.fetch nor network.unrestricted is requested
Add a network permission if the plugin needs requests, or remove networkHosts otherwise.
Declare bare hosts only, for example api.example.com; do not include schemes, paths, ports, or wildcards.
Use a comparable version string such as 1.2.3 or 0.14.67-beta.
This plugin requires TinaIDE %1$s or later, but the current host version is %2$s
Upgrade TinaIDE, or install a plugin version that supports the current host.
This host does not support the command ID, and this plugin type cannot register it at runtime: %1$s
Use a host whitelist command, or register the same command ID from a script/hybrid plugin with tina.commands.register().
Menu item references a command the host does not support: %1$s (location %2$s)
Menu item references a plugin command that is not declared in contributions.commands: %1$s (location %2$s)
Declare the same command ID in contributions.commands and register it at runtime with tina.commands.register().
Custom plugin commands were detected, but manifest does not declare command.execute: %1$s
Add command.execute to permissions or optionalPermissions, then reload the plugin.
Menu item uses a when expression that is not supported yet: %1$s (location %2$s)
Use one of the currently supported when expressions, or remove the condition.
editor/toolbar is declared, but the host has not wired that menu surface yet
Use editor/context for now, or wait for host toolbar support.
panels is declared, but the host does not provide a panel entry point yet
Keeping the manifest declaration is fine; enable the UI entry after the host exposes panel support.
File icon rule is missing icon (entry %1$d)
File icon rule is missing extension or filename matchers (entry %1$d)
File icon asset does not exist: %1$s
Use a valid relative icon path and declare at least one extension or filename matcher.
This path is a system-sensitive file and access is not allowed
Path %1$s is not within allowed range (guest)
Only access to %1$s directories is allowed
Please ensure the path format is correct
Path %1$s is not within allowed range (host)
Only access to app private directory and workspace files is allowed
File "%1$s" has been modified by an external program.\n\nYour version may differ from the version on disk.
University of Science and Technology of China mirror
Ubuntu official source (not recommended, prone to DNS hijacking)
Real LSP mode requires an active LSP connection in the editor first
Clone timed out. The repo may be large or the network is slow. Please try again later.
Merge conflicts detected. Please resolve the following files:
Permission denied. Please check repo permissions or SSH/token config.\n\n%1$s
Network error. Please check network/proxy settings.\n\n%1$s
Push rejected (non-fast-forward). Please pull first or confirm force push.\n\n%1$s
Merge conflict. Please resolve conflicts and try again.\n\n%1$s
Repository not found or no access.\n\n%1$s
HTTPS credentials required. Please configure token/password and retry.\n\n%1$s
Path of current file relative to project
Current-file variables require an opened file
Source-file variables require a specified source file
Note: program execution has no timeout limit
LLVM debugger, works best with Clang
run_configs.json migrated automatically: schema %1$d -> %2$d, normalized %3$d config(s)
run_configs.json selected config was invalid and has been reset to the first entry
By clicking Start, you agree to
Clear all bookmarks for the current project?
Exported build artifact to project directory: %1$s
Clang is unavailable. Trying to auto-install the LLVM toolchain...
Cached artifact failed to launch (%1$s), rebuilding…
Rebuild did not resolve the issue; please check sources/environment
Plugin projects do not support debug yet. Use Run to package and hot-install.
Cannot read project directory. Please check storage permissions.
Clang is unavailable and auto-installation failed. Please check your network connection and retry, or install the LLVM toolchain manually in Settings.
Custom compiler selected, but the C/C++ compiler paths are incomplete.

Custom compiler is unavailable:
C: %1$s
C++: %2$s
Please make sure the paths exist and are executable in the PRoot environment.
Project directory is empty. Please add source files (.c, .cpp, etc.).
GCC is not installed or unavailable. Please install the GCC (gcc/g++) toolchain in Settings and retry.
LLDB is not installed and auto-installation failed. Please redeploy the dev environment in Settings or retry later.
No supported source files detected. Directory contents: %1$s\nSupported file types: .c, .cc, .cpp, .cxx, CMakeLists.txt
PRoot environment is initializing. Please retry later (it auto-installs on first launch/update).
PRoot environment needs an update. Restart the app and wait for the background update to finish, then compile again.
PRoot environment is not installed. Open the app and wait for the first-launch auto-install to complete, then compile again.
No configuration needed for single-file projects
Not a valid C/C++ source file: %1$s
No configuration step needed for Makefile projects
Failed to get current editor file: %1$s
LLDB is unavailable (not installed or missing Python bindings). Trying to install/repair...
PRoot environment is initializing. Please retry later.
PRoot environment needs an update. Please restart the app and wait for the background update to finish.
PRoot environment is not installed. Open the app and wait for auto-installation to complete.
PTY produced no output bytes (exitCode=%1$d, command=%2$s)
Compilation complete. Ready to run in terminal.
\n[Program exited, code: %1$d]\nPress Enter to close this screen...
GUI mode could not find a runnable shared library output (.so). Please check your target type and build settings.
GUI mode requires a .so output, current artifact: %1$s
Hint: please check your network connection, or install the toolchain manually in Settings
Warning: no file open, using auto-detect mode
Warning: no runnable build output found
Warning: current file is not a C/C++ source file, using auto-detect mode
Warning: specified source file is invalid, using auto-detect mode
Crash details are shown below. Tap Copy to copy the full log.
The Native/GUI program you ran crashed in an isolated process. The TinaIDE main screen should not be affected. Crash details are shown below. Tap Copy to copy the full log.
This is a crash log from the user project or runtime program. It will not be uploaded to TinaIDE servers. You can copy it or share it with the project author for local troubleshooting.
No upload status has been detected yet. The app will check crash logs again after restart.
Added to the background upload queue and will upload automatically when network is available.
Trying to upload the crash log.
This crash log will not be uploaded. The log is still kept locally.
Upload has not succeeded yet. The local log is kept and will be retried automatically.
Upload failed. The log is still kept locally and can be copied or shared with developers.
Manually set compiler paths and flags
No breakpoints. Tap a line number to add one.
No call stack (hit a breakpoint or tap Pause to view).
No variables (hit a breakpoint or tap Pause to view).
You can install the debugger later when needed
Load and display images rendered by SDL3 or other GUI libraries
When off, no editor LSP session is attached, so you can inspect Tree-sitter highlighting only
When off, built-in CMake semantic completion and highlighting are disabled for Tree-sitter-only verification
The editor below is the highlighting preview. Selecting a language loads its sample file, and Reanalyze rebuilds the file and refreshes highlighting.
Use this page to isolate Tree-sitter highlighting. Turn off the editor LSP master switch to keep Tree-sitter as the only highlighter.
If you only want to verify the impact of the built-in CMake LSP, keep the master switch on and disable only the built-in CMake LSP.
Recommended files: CMakeLists.txt, *.cmake, and CMake scripts containing variables, targets, functions, and macros.
Master switch: when off, no extra diagnostic logs are emitted (reduces performance overhead/log volume)
Record compile_commands lookup, reuse, and generation summary to confirm which compile database LSP actually uses
Record the compile database summary, standard library header clues, and sysroot clues seen before clangd starts to diagnose false errors in SDL3/CMake projects
Record editor touch/scroll sequences (including MOVE_NO_SCROLL/UP velocity/offset) to diagnose "scroll stuck/no inertia"
Record gesture consumption in Compose (Drawer/bottom panel, etc.) to trace ACTION_CANCEL source
Step-by-step test of compilation stages to quickly locate compile/link issues
Uses the stable baseline package with LLVM/CMake Android exec patches and does not add tina-exec.
Tap a button above to start a test...
Test 3: Check ld.lld path returned by clang++
❌ Error: object file missing, run "Compile only" first
❌ Critical link files missing, skipping execution
Note: this test forces direct exec clang++ and does not go through NativeExecutableRunner\'s linker64 wrapper.
Test B: full compile + link (direct clang++)
Comparison hint: compare this test\'s exit code and errors with items 4 and 5.
Note: this uses -### and does not actually execute linking.
Baseline compile-only comparison (cache dir + absolute paths)
CMake-like try_compile comparison (API 28 + Scratch dir + relative depfile)
Path alias check: /data/user/0 and /data/data are equivalent (same dev+ino)
Path alias check: current path and alias path are not equivalent (different dev+ino)
Path alias check: unable to verify dev+ino (path missing or permission denied)
1. If the baseline passes but the CMake-like current-native-runner still fails, the issue is more likely the CMake try_compile shape itself rather than the wrapper alone.
2. If the CMake-like current-native-runner succeeds but wrapper-direct fails, the main issue is likely in the wrapper chain; consider falling back to linker64 as the CMake compiler launcher.
3. If only the shell shim succeeds while current and wrapper both fail, the issue is more likely the exec/preload combination with real clang.
4. If all CMake-like cases fail, the issue is more likely API 28 flags, relative depfiles, the scratch working directory, or ROM restrictions.
14. Real SDL3 CMake project loop (build + clangd)
15. Run project loop comparison (single-file / CMake / SDL3)
Test AI message rendering, Markdown formatting, tool calls, etc.
Load image outputs from SDL3 or other GUI libraries, or generate a demo image to verify the display pipeline.
No image loaded. Pick one or generate a demo image.
File not found, please check the path
Image decode failed, please verify the file format
When enabled, registration does not require verification code (use only when email service is unavailable)
Output internal logs like editor onFling (to confirm fling triggers and parameters)
Output pinch focus sampling details (pointer count, stable pointer count, sampling basis)
Output fling start/end logs (axis, offset, max range)
Theme preview, syntax highlighting, LSP and other test entries
Force re-extract precompiled libraries (SDL3, etc.) from assets
Switch backend URL and test connectivity
Canceling will stop the current installation. You can restart it later.
Clear all log cache? This action cannot be undone.
Zsh is not installed.\n\nInstall now? (Requires network and the PRoot Linux environment)

A TinaIDE issue diagnostic package will be generated and sent to the server. Please confirm that you want to share the following information.

Included:
• TinaIDE structured logs (Timber)
• Current app-process logcat only (no global logcat)
• App/device info, TinaIDE host crash logs, and installation diagnostics
• Project build logs, which may contain project paths, compiler output, or dependency names
• PRoot/rootfs/Linux environment logs, which may contain repositories, installed packages, or command output
• export_manifest.txt with the collection policy

Not included:
• Source files or complete project files
• Global logcat
• User GUI/SDL/Native/NDK program crash tombstones

If you do not want to share these runtime environment logs, cancel and use Export logs for local sharing.
For HTTPS repositories on GitHub/Gitee/GitLab, etc.
Add a token here if private repositories require authentication
Open the file(s) to resolve conflicts, then mark as resolved, or abort merge/rebase.
Resolve conflicts and stage changes, then tap "Finish merge" to create a merge commit.
Resolve conflicts and stage changes, then tap "Continue rebase" to proceed; or tap "Skip" to skip the current commit.
All conflict files are resolved. Continue to finish merge/rebase.
Select files to mark (leave empty to select all)
Force pushing will overwrite remote history and may cause others\' commits to be lost. Continue?
You can initialize with git init
This project is not a Git repository. Initialize now?
This project is not a Git repository
Remove remote \"%1$s\"?\n\nThis only removes the local Git configuration for the current project. The remote repository will not be deleted.
Learn the basics of TinaIDE and how to use it
Learn how to create a C/C++ project
Create a plugin project from a template, run to hot-install, package .tinaplug, and verify with install preflight
Find the current settings sections, common options, and troubleshooting entry points
Understand rootfs import, profiles, MT access, and storage cleanup entry points
Understand autosave, backup, APK export, native dependency paths, and build flag configuration
Boost productivity with smart code completion
Understand font, theme, whitespace, rainbow bracket, indentation, and hardware acceleration settings
Learn how to build and run projects
Understand optimization, runtime modes, toolchains, sysroot, and environment redeploy
Understand clangd, local LSP behavior, and remote LSP sync and connection settings
Configure a remote LSP server for better performance
Configure rsync daemon for project sync
Run commands with the built-in terminal
Understand theme, font, backend, shell, Linux tools, and interaction settings
Diagnose terminal backend, shell, dev packages, Zsh, and locale issues
Understand HTTPS credentials, SSH keys, the default key, and host bindings
Understand access modes, channel management, models, and tool-related options
Understand app theme and debug toolbar position settings
Understand customizable actions, conflict detection, and restoring default shortcuts
Understand installed packages, platform states, update checks, detail pages, and batch uninstall
Understand installed plugin management, file-based installs, plugin logs, theme plugins, and LSP dependencies
Understand version info, community links, log export/upload, crash upload, and open-source licenses
Understand diagnostics, backend debugging, test tools, and bundled package reinstall
Understand feedback types, length limits, submit throttling, and automatic device info
Understand feedback, settings, about, download history, and local data entries
Known issues and limitations of the current version
This document is under construction. Stay tuned.
If you have any questions, use feedback or GitHub Issues to reach us.
Use GCC to save space, suitable for devices with limited storage
TinaIDE application log files for diagnostics.
Database is not ready. Try again shortly.
Get available code actions (quick fixes, etc.)
Jump to the declaration of a symbol
Notify the server that document content changed
Notify the server that the document was closed
Notify the server that the document was opened
Notify the server that the document was saved
Highlight occurrences of the same symbol
Get all symbols in the document
Jump to implementations of an interface/abstract method
Get inline hints like parameter names and types
Check whether the symbol can be renamed
Find all references to the symbol
Rename symbols such as variables and functions
Get semantic tokens for the entire document
Get semantic tokens for the specified range
Jump to the type definition of a variable
Toolchain incomplete: missing clang resource directory (checked version: %1$s)
Toolchain installation failed: %1$s\n\nSuggestion: check your network connection, or switch mirrors in Settings and retry.
Toolchain installation failed: PRoot runtime error (proot failed to start).\n%1$s
Toolchain incomplete: missing C++ standard library headers (%1$s)
Debugger will retry installation on first debug
Installation already in progress; skipping duplicate request
Toolchain manifest is outdated or incomplete; falling back to scan
PRoot guest toolchain is not fully installed
Android NDK Sysroot already installed, skipping
Critical toolchain files missing; trying to install/repair from network
Toolchain install finished but critical files are still missing; considered failed
Toolchain install finished but critical files are missing. LSP will be unavailable. Please check your network connection and retry.
Toolchain status: ready via manifest fast check (clangd=%1$s)
Toolchain files are complete via filesystem check; skipping all network installs
Toolchain repair failed. LSP will be unavailable. Please check your network connection and retry.
startToolchainRepair called but state is not NeedsToolchainRepair
Recommended for beginners. One-click install of the complete Clang dev environment
Includes the Clang compiler and full toolchain; suitable for most scenarios
Clangd (LSP), LLVM, C++ standard library, CMake, Ninja, Make, Git
Output is shown in the build log panel at the bottom
Run in a separate terminal, supports interactive input
Run inside GUI host with real-time rendering (output must be .so)
Force landscape mode, ideal for most games and graphics programs
Show a draggable real-time log window during fullscreen GUI rendering for stdout/stderr output
Floating log feature is under development, stay tuned
Press back again to exit, or tap the floating ball
Missing shared library path to load
GUI run requires a shared library (.so). Current target is not loadable: %1$s
GUI run requires a CMake shared library target (.so). Change the CMake target to add_library(... SHARED ...), or switch to Terminal mode to run an executable.
GUI mode automatically selected shared library target: %1$s
Hint: must export tina_gui_render_argb32. Optionally implement tina_gui_init, tina_gui_shutdown, tina_gui_on_touch, tina_gui_on_key. See tina_gui.h for details.
Main shared library does not exist or is unreadable: %1$s
Failed to scan shared library: %1$s, reason: %2$s
Detected dependencies on both SDL2 and SDL3; cannot determine runtime version.
Detected SDL%1$d dependency, but %2$s was not found in package-manager installed runtime library paths. Please install the SDL%1$d package first.
Detected installed SDL%1$d package(s) (%2$s), but %3$s is missing. Please reinstall from the package manager.
Missing SDL launch parameters; cannot start GUI.
SDL runtime library does not exist or is unreadable: %1$s
Invalid SDL major version parameter: %1$d (only 2 or 3 are supported)
Select the toolchain version for this configuration
When enabled, suggestions are shown only if the case of your typed prefix matches
HTTPS auth and SSH key management
Export app runtime logs for diagnostics
Microsoft C++ style, 4-space indentation, Allman braces
When a .clang-format file exists in the project, the project configuration takes precedence
Choose a Linux distribution to install
Linux distro self-check failed. Reinstall or clean the environment in Settings, then try again.
Show parameter name hints inline (does not insert into text)
Overlay semantic tokens from the language server on top of syntax highlighting (experimental)
Use the language server folding ranges as the folding source (experimental)
Show function signature and current parameter when typing ( or ,
Please configure the server host first
Connect to a remote LSP server via WebSocket (e.g. clangd on your PC)
Tip: run an LSP proxy on your PC (e.g. lsp-ws-proxy). Device → PC: use LAN IP (or run adb reverse tcp:PORT tcp:PORT and then use 127.0.0.1). Emulator → host: use 10.0.2.2.
Zero configuration, suitable for small to medium projects
Incremental transfer, suitable for large projects
Path mapping for remote clangd; can be left empty for built-in sync
Enter a file:// URI or an absolute path (e.g. C:\\path or /path), or leave empty
Choose the shell used by terminal (restart terminal to apply)
Upload an anonymous diagnostics package with device and runtime environment information
View PRoot session/error logs (for troubleshooting install and runtime issues)
Modern shell with powerful completion and themes

Sync project to remote server?

Project: %1$s
Files: %2$d
Total size: %3$s
Select terminal environment (restart terminal to apply)
Use Linux if PRoot installed, otherwise use system
Full Linux dev environment with apt/gcc/python etc.
Run directly on Android, can execute NDK-compiled binaries
Terminal mode changed, please restart terminal to apply
PRoot not installed, will use system mode
Cannot set a bookmark on this line
Shell type changed. Restart the terminal to apply.
System environment detected. Cannot auto-install Zsh; please install the PRoot Linux environment first or switch to Bash/Sh.
To ensure efficient compilation and debugging, we need to set up the underlying toolchain. The system will automatically match the best version for your device architecture.
You can change toolchain settings anytime in Settings
%1$d files have unsaved changes.\n\nSave before exiting?
set() command is missing a variable name
Make sure the target is defined before calling this command
Submit bug reports or feature requests
Please describe your issue or suggestion in detail
We read every feedback carefully. Thank you for your support!
Submission failed, please try again later
Too many submissions, please wait %1$d seconds
Title must be at least %1$d characters
Content must be at least %1$d characters
Network connection failed, please check your network and try again
Server-backed interactions are not bundled in the open-source build. Rating, comments, and reports are unavailable.
Publishing is not bundled in the open-source build. Plugin browsing, downloads, favorites, and download history remain available.
Plugin download URL is not configured: %1$s
Tap a star to submit your rating
Write your feedback. Submitting again updates your comment.
No comments yet. Be the first to write one.
Report submitted. We will review it soon.
Are you sure you want to clear all plugin logs? This action cannot be undone.
Are you sure you want to clear logs for %1$s? This action cannot be undone.
Go to "Market" on the home screen to browse and install packages
Warning: The following packages depend on this package:
💡 Tip: Reopen your file to use the newly installed package (header completion and compilation)
Are you sure you want to clear all install history?
Try fast swiping to test fling effect
Pay attention to diagonal fast swipes - check for \"lag\" or single-direction scrolling
Please add implementation(\"com.itsaky.androidide.treesitter:tree-sitter-xxx\") to app dependencies, then Sync/Build.
Exciting plugins are on the way, stay tuned
Development packages are on the way, stay tuned
Describe what\'s new in this version (optional)
Tutorial links stay in the learning flow, while help links open in Help Center.
Opens the matching Help Center article
Learn the basics of TinaIDE from scratch
Tips and features to boost your coding efficiency
Compile, run and debug your projects
Quick tour of TinaIDE\'s main features
Learn the interface layout and basic operations
Master code editing, undo/redo and shortcuts
Learn CMakeLists.txt configuration and build options
Commit, view history and manage branches
All your projects are displayed here. Tap to open and edit.
Tap this button to create a new C/C++ project
Browse and download various plugins and packages here
Find tutorials to help you learn TinaIDE
Manage feedback, settings, about, and download history
Would you like to start the guided tour? We\'ll show you the main features of TinaIDE.
Congratulations on completing the guided tour!\n\nWant to learn more tips for using TinaIDE? Check out more tutorials in the Tutorial section.
Private identity and commercial features have been moved out. Feedback, logs, plugins, package downloads, favorites, and download history remain available.
Compile with NDK sysroot, enables Android APIs
Try switching to the corresponding page or expanding the control, then continue.
tool_call_id is empty, cannot submit tool result
AI not ready (please check server connection)
Tool call missing id, cannot execute
Tool call missing name, cannot execute
Tool execution context is not initialized. Open a project and wait for initialization to finish.
Execution callbacks not available. Please ensure project context is initialized.
Project root not set. Please initialize project context first.
Failed to stop execution: %1$s (execution may not exist or already completed)
Execution not found: %1$s (may have been cleaned up or never existed)
Execution output not found: %1$s (execution may not exist or output not available yet)
Failed to navigate to run output: %1$s
Failed to navigate to build log: %1$s
Source and destination paths are required
End line must be >= start line
Editor callbacks not available. Please ensure editor context is initialized.
Start line (%1$d) must be less than or equal to end line (%2$d)
Failed to format code. The file may not support formatting or clang-format is not available.
Method name and code are required
Branch or commit SHA (default: repository\'s default branch)
Brief description of what the code does
Clean build artifacts before building (default: false)
Code to extract into the method
Directory to analyze relative to project root (default: \'.\' for entire project)
Directory to search in relative to project root (default: \'.\' for entire project)
File name or pattern to search for (supports * and ? wildcards, e.g., \'*.cpp\', \'main.*\', \'test_*.h\')
Include info and hints in results
Maximum number of lines to return (default: 500, max: 2000)
Maximum number of results to return
Maximum number of results to return (default: 10, max: 20)
Maximum number of results to return (default: 10, max: 30)
Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)
Optional arguments passed to executable test targets.
Optional command-line arguments passed to the executable
Optional execution ID to get errors from a specific build (obtained from build_project). If not provided, returns all current build errors.
Optional test class or target name. For C/C++ projects this is treated as a target name.
Optional test method or filter argument passed to executable test targets.
Optional test package or target group. For C/C++ projects this can be treated as a target name.
Optional test target name. For CMake/Make this can be \'test\' or a specific executable test target.
Optional working directory used when launching the executable
Path to the file to format (relative to project root)
Search query (e.g., \'cmake tutorial\', \'clang-format configuration\', \'C++ best practices\')
Specific build target name (optional, uses current selected config if not specified)
The content to write to the file
The destination file or directory path
The directory path to analyze relative to project root (default: \'.\' for root)
The directory path to list (relative to project root or absolute)
The directory path to search in (default: project root)
The ending line number to delete (default: same as start_line)
The execution ID to check (obtained from run_project or build_project)
The execution ID to get output from (obtained from run_project or build_project)
The execution ID to stop (obtained from run_project or build_project)
The file or directory path to delete
The file path (relative to project root or absolute)
The file path to clear diagnostics for
The file path to get diagnostics for (omit for all files)
The file path to get outline for
The file path to read (relative to project root or absolute)
The file path to write (relative to project root or absolute)
The line number of the symbol (optional)
The line number to replace (starts from 1)
The new code to replace the selection
The new content for the line
The run configuration name to use (optional, uses current selected config if not specified)
The search query (text or regex pattern)
The source file or directory path
The starting line number to delete (starts from 1)
The symbol name to find references for
The symbol type (class, function, variable, etc.)
The text to find and replace
Type of code element (class, function, property)
Whether the search is case sensitive
Whether the search is case sensitive (default: false)
Whether to create parent directories if they don\'t exist
Whether to overwrite if destination exists
Whether to replace all occurrences (default: false, replaces only first)
Whether to show files or only directories (default: true)
Whether to treat query as regex pattern

Search for code patterns across the project. Supports regex patterns, file filtering (*.kt, *.java), case sensitivity, and result limiting. Use this to find specific code snippets, function calls, or patterns in the codebase.

Locate the definition of a code symbol (class, function, variable, interface, enum, constant). Returns file path, line number, signature, and documentation. Useful for understanding where a symbol is defined and its structure.
Find all locations where a symbol is referenced or used in the project. Distinguishes between definitions and usages. Helpful for impact analysis, refactoring, and understanding code dependencies.
Extract the structural outline of a file showing classes, methods, properties, and their hierarchical relationships. Provides a high-level overview of file organization without reading full content.

Get code diagnostics (errors, warnings, hints) for a file or the entire project. Returns detailed information about syntax errors, type errors, linting issues, and code quality problems with line numbers and messages.
Get all diagnostics for the entire project. Returns a comprehensive list of all errors, warnings, and issues across all files. Useful for getting an overview of project health.
Clear cached diagnostics for a specific file. Use this to refresh diagnostic information after making code changes.
Retrieve the complete content of the currently open file in the editor, including file name, language type, and full text. Use this to understand the context of the current working file.

Get the code snippet currently selected by the user in the editor, including file name, language, line range (start/end), and the selected text. Useful for analyzing or modifying specific code sections.
Insert code at the current cursor position in the editor without replacing existing content. Use this to add new code snippets, imports, or functions at the cursor location.
Replace the currently selected code in the editor with new code. Use this for refactoring, fixing bugs, or updating specific code sections that the user has highlighted.

Run the C/C++ project using the current run configuration. Compiles the project if needed and executes the binary. Returns execution status, output, and any errors. Use this to test the application or verify changes work correctly.

Build/compile the C/C++ project using CMake or Makefile. Compiles source files and generates the executable binary. Returns build output, success status, and any compilation errors. Use before running to ensure code compiles correctly.
Stop a running execution (project run or build) by its execution ID. Terminates the process and cancels the operation. Use this to cancel long-running or stuck processes.
Get the current status of a running or completed execution by its ID. Returns status (pending, running, success, failed, cancelled, timeout) and helps track long-running operations.

Get the output and error output of a completed or running execution by its ID. Returns stdout, stderr, exit code, and current status. Use this to retrieve detailed execution results and diagnose issues.

Get all build errors and warnings from the current project or a specific build execution. Returns error count, warning count, and detailed error messages with file locations and line numbers. Use this to diagnose compilation issues and fix code problems.
Open and focus the run output panel in the IDE. Use this after running a project to show the user where to view the execution results and program output.
Open and focus the build log panel in the IDE. Use this after building a project to show the user where to view compilation logs, errors, and warnings.
Run tests for the current C/C++ project. Prefer a test build target such as CMake/Ninja \'test\', or run an executable test target when available. Use this after code changes to verify behavior.

Read the complete content of a file from the file system. Supports both relative (to project root) and absolute paths. Returns file content, size, line count, and encoding. Large files are automatically truncated.
Write content to a file, creating it if it doesn\'t exist or overwriting if it does. Automatically creates parent directories if needed. Use for generating new files or updating existing ones.
List files and directories in a path. Supports recursive traversal, file pattern filtering (*.kt, *.java), and hidden file inclusion. Returns file paths, types (file/directory), and sizes.
Delete a file or directory from the file system
Create a new directory in the file system
Move or rename a file or directory
Copy a file or directory to a new location
Get information about a file or directory (size, modified time, permissions, etc.)
Find and replace text in a file. Supports replacing first occurrence or all occurrences. Automatically syncs with open editor tabs.
Replace the content of a specific line in a file. Line numbers start from 1. Automatically syncs with open editor tabs.
Insert a new line before or after a specific line number in a file. Line numbers start from 1. Automatically syncs with open editor tabs.
Delete one or more lines from a file. Can delete a single line or a range of lines. Line numbers start from 1. Automatically syncs with open editor tabs.

Get the directory structure of the project. Shows folders and files in a tree format with configurable depth. Useful for understanding project organization, locating source files, headers, and build configurations. Automatically excludes build artifacts and common temporary directories.

Find files by name or pattern in the project. Supports wildcards (* and ?) and case-insensitive search. Returns file paths relative to project root. Useful for locating source files, headers, or configuration files. Faster than list_files for finding specific files.

Count lines of code in the project or specific directory. Breaks down by file type (C, C++, H, CMake, etc.) and shows total lines, code lines, comment lines, and blank lines. Useful for project metrics, understanding codebase size, and tracking code complexity.

Format C/C++ code using clang-format. Automatically applies consistent code style including indentation, spacing, braces, and line breaks. Supports formatting entire files or specific code ranges. Essential for maintaining code quality and readability.

Extract selected code into a new method/function. Automatically detects parameters and return type. Useful for refactoring long methods and improving code reusability. Requires method name and the code to extract.

Generate documentation comments for code elements (classes, functions, properties). Creates KDoc/JavaDoc format comments with parameter descriptions, return value, and usage examples. Improves code maintainability.

Search GitHub for code, repositories, issues, or users. Useful for finding code examples, libraries, solutions to problems, or checking if similar projects exist. Returns repository names, descriptions, stars, and relevant code snippets.

Search the web for information, documentation, tutorials, or solutions to problems using various search engines (Baidu, Bing, Google, DuckDuckGo). Useful for finding up-to-date information, API documentation, error solutions, or learning resources. Returns search results with titles, snippets, and URLs.

Read file content from a GitHub repository. Useful for examining code examples, reading documentation, or studying implementations from open source projects. Supports reading from specific branches or commits.
✅ No diagnostics found - project is clean!
Navigated to run output panel. The run output tab is now visible and focused.
Navigated to build log panel. The build log tab is now visible and focused.
C++ syntax highlight scroll stress test
Use very long C++ code to test the impact of highlighting/folding/rainbow brackets on scrolling
Tap the bottom-right button to expand the menu: New project, Import from Git, Import from local
Linux system "%1$s" is missing required shell: %2$s
Positive integer; must be incremented on each reinstall, otherwise the system rejects the update
Terminal template requires an executable file.
APK signing failed. Retry, or switch to custom signing if it keeps failing. Details: %1$s
Allow TinaIDE to install unknown apps, then tap Install again
No system package installer is available
File sharing provider is misconfigured. Reinstall TinaIDE or export logs for diagnosis.
No .so files found. Please build the project first.
No libmain.so found. Please build the project first.
No executable file found. Please build the project first.
Package name must look like com.example.app; each segment starts with a lowercase letter and contains only a-z, 0-9 or _, with at least one dot.
Use the bundled key for quick export
Import a keystore or generate a new one
Import or generate a keystore first
Unable to open the keystore. Check the file and keystore password: %1$s
The key password is incorrect, or the alias does not reference a signing key
Only the exported APK Manifest will be updated. The template itself no longer carries a fixed permission set.
Declared only on Android %1$d and below

\"Manage all files\" is a restricted system setting. It is not granted automatically on first request; the user must enable it manually in App info → Permissions, otherwise external storage access will fail at runtime.
Required when registering with third-party SDKs (WeChat, AMap, etc.). Long-press to copy.
Optional. Defaults to the template icon; a square PNG looks crispest.
Optional. Use this when your app loads .so files through dlopen or plugin lookup; selected files are always packaged into the APK and override auto-resolved libraries with the same name.
No shared library files (.so) were selected
There is no data here yet
Please check your network and try again

Please summarize the key content of the above conversation, including:
1. Main issues discussed
2. Work completed
3. Problems encountered and solutions
4. Pending tasks

Please summarize concisely while preserving technical details.
Project template file does not exist: %1$s
APK export template file does not exist: %1$s
LSP server %1$s must declare fileExtensions or filePatterns
Only stdio LSP servers are currently supported: %1$s
LSP server %1$s must declare a startup command
Invalid LSP toolchain type: %1$s. System packages must use system; apt/apk are no longer accepted.
LSP system toolchain %1$s must declare packages or packagesByManager
Build with debug info for development and troubleshooting
Build optimized artifacts for final runs and performance testing
Revealed the build artifacts directory in the file tree
This project does not have a build artifacts directory yet
The current project is not a CMake project
Cleaning the build directory and reconfiguring the CMake cache...
Cleaned the build directory and reconfigured the CMake cache
Failed to clean and reconfigure the CMake cache
No editor performance snapshot is available.
The project is being compiled and will run shortly.
The execution is queued and waiting to start.
The execution is currently in progress.
The execution failed. Check the execution result for details.
The execution was cancelled by user.
The test target is being prepared and will run shortly.
No files found matching the pattern.
The code has been formatted using clang-format.
Changes have been applied to the editor.
